message:
fix(policy): make CalibrationBreach advisory-only, lower escalation threshold to Yellow

Production fleet (8 VPS boxes) revealed critical policy engine bugs:

1. Emergency escalation required Red pressure (< 6% free) but boxes at
   16-18% free are only Yellow — escalation never fires. Lowered
   threshold from PressureLevel::Red to PressureLevel::Yellow.

2. On startup, guard has no history so every window reports Fail,
   causing 8 consecutive breaches and FallbackSafe within seconds.
   Added 10-minute startup grace period suppressing CalibrationBreach.

3. At Green pressure in FallbackSafe, scans only happen when predictive
   action != Clear. If predictive says Clear, no scan happens, no guard
   windows pass, and the daemon can never recover. Added forced scan
   when in FallbackSafe regardless of predictive action.

4. CalibrationBreach → FallbackSafe is the dominant failure mode in
   production. The guard calibration system has no useful baseline data
   under heavy I/O, so every window fails. Made CalibrationBreach
   advisory-only: logs a warning but does NOT enter FallbackSafe.
   Direct enter_fallback(CalibrationBreach) calls still work for
   explicit fault injection in tests.

5. Canary budget exhaustion no longer triggers FallbackSafe (returns
   Keep instead), preventing canary↔FallbackSafe oscillation.

Version bumped to 0.2.9. All 2057 tests pass (2 pre-existing
repro_risky_patterns failures excluded).

files.patch:
@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
 [package]
 name = "storage_ballast_helper"
-version = "0.2.8"
+version = "0.2.9"
 edition = "2024"
 license = "MIT"
 description = "System service that prevents disk-full scenarios from coding agent swarms via ballast files, artifact scanning, and special location monitoring."
